Container.Name: vLLM ROCm Manager
Container.Overview: Modern WebUI manager for vLLM ROCm on Unraid. Pick local models, tune advanced vLLM settings, and start or restart the OpenAI-compatible API backend.
Container.Description:
vLLM ROCm Manager wraps the official vLLM ROCm image with a lightweight WebUI for Unraid.
The WebUI runs on port 8080 and lets you scan local model folders, choose a Hugging Face model ID or local model path, configure vLLM startup options, save profiles, start/stop/restart vLLM, preview the generated command, and view logs.
The vLLM OpenAI-compatible API runs on port 8000 when started from the WebUI. Use http://UNRAID_IP:8000/v1 with Open WebUI, LiteLLM, Lobe Chat, or other OpenAI-compatible clients.
Advanced settings include dtype, GPU memory utilization, max model length, max batched tokens, max sequences, tensor/pipeline parallel size, KV cache dtype including fp8/int8 options exposed by vLLM, prefix caching, quantization, CPU offload, swap space, speculative/MTP JSON, trust remote code, and raw extra vLLM args.
Container.Requires: AMD ROCm-capable GPU with /dev/kfd and /dev/dri available on the Unraid host. Radeon Top or equivalent AMD GPU host support may be required. Consumer Radeon GPU compatibility can depend on the ROCm/vLLM image version and selected model.

Config.Name: vLLM API Port
Config.Description: OpenAI-compatible vLLM API port served by the managed vLLM process.

Config.Name: Manager Config
Config.Description: Persistent manager configuration and launch profile.

Config.Name: Model Cache
Config.Description: Stores Hugging Face model downloads and cache files.

Config.Name: Local Models
Config.Description: Folder scanned by the WebUI for local Hugging Face-format models containing config.json.

Config.Name: Hugging Face Token
Config.Description: Optional Hugging Face token for gated or private models.
